steps.title: Use acute angle sum
steps.content:
A right triangle has one $90^{\circ}$ angle, so the two acute angles add to:
$$180^{\circ}-90^{\circ}=90^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Subtract the given angle
steps.content:
One acute angle is $23^{\circ}$, so the other acute angle is:
$$90^{\circ}-23^{\circ}=67^{\circ}$$
Disregard the degree symbol when entering it. The answer is 67.
